title: The Anaesthetic Ketamine as Treatment for Patients With Severe Acute Brain Injury

skipped_criteria: Admitted to NICU with diagnosis of TBI, aSAH, or ICH: schema has presumed_tbi and presumed_intracranial_hemorrhage but no field for aneurysmal subarachnoid haemorrhage specifically; partially encoded via available bool fields.
Planned for surgery with a supratentorial craniotomy or craniectomy: no schema field for planned surgical procedure type.
Expected to continue sedation and mechanical ventilation after surgery: no schema field for post-operative sedation or ventilation status.
Neither patient nor next of kin understand Danish or English: no schema field for language comprehension.
Known allergy to S-ketamine: no schema field for drug allergies.
Wake-up call to occur immediately after surgery: no schema field for post-operative sedation plan.
Active anti-psychotic treatment before admission: no schema field for prior medication use.
Current abuse of ketamine: no schema field for substance abuse history.
Decision to withdraw active treatment: no schema field for goals-of-care or treatment withdrawal status.
ICH secondary to a known brain tumour at the time of inclusion: no schema field for aetiology of intracranial haemorrhage or brain tumour history.
